story: Bobby is bored in the car, thumping the seat and asking, “Are we there yet?” Mama promises to wake him when they get there. He naps; she wakes him at a roadside place with animals and shiny stalls: “We’re here.” He sulks about wanting to go “there.” Ice cream smooths it over, and they head back to the car. His stomach aches; Mama says it will pass and offers his tablet. As she buckles in, she checks her phone map and spots a rest area with a playground about 25 minutes ahead, deciding they’ll stop there. The wheels roll.
implausible_knowledge: As she buckles in, she checks her phone map and spots a rest area with a playground about 25 minutes ahead, deciding they’ll stop there.
question: As they start driving again and Mama offers the tablet, what will Bobby most likely do during the next stretch of the drive?
answer_with_knowledge: Wait the stop at the playground.
answer_without_knowledge: Take the tablet to watch the video and keep asking, “Are we there yet?”
